news.title: AI buildout gives tech investors new reasons to watch bond market
news.summary: Tech giants are depleting cash reserves and raising debt in their ambitious data center buildouts. The dynamic is forcing investors to watch interest rates.

news.title: The budget airline model in the U.S. is running out of runway
news.summary: Spirit's bankruptcy as United and Delta fly high suggests there's more to success in airline industry than low fares, and failure isn't about jet fuel spikes.

news.title: SNAP restrictions could change what shoppers buy — and food giants are watching
news.summary: SNAP food restrictions are spreading to more states, pressuring major food and beverage companies. Consumers shift spending away from soda, candy and processed foods.

news.title: Musk's SpaceX stake is worth over $1 trillion. Here are the other billionaire shareholders
news.summary: SpaceX shares were up 37% after its historic debut last week. The company offered shares at a set price of $135. SpaceX shares are now valued at more than $1 billion.

news.title: Memory crisis hits such extremes that 'even Apple can't be safe'
news.summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook called an "unsustainable" memory shortage. Apple appears poised to take the rare step of increasing prices to deal with the shortage.

news.title: Americans’ 401(k) balances hit record levels last year. See how you compare.
news.summary: Vanguard’s latest “How America Saves” report says 2013 was a very good year for most workplace retirement savers.

news.title: ‘We are habitually frugal’: My wife and I have money. How do we help our children without ruining their independence?
news.summary: “Partly due to some mental-health issues, they may continue to go through life living paycheck to paycheck, or worse,” she says.

news.title: Tesla booted from the ‘Magnificent Seven’ by a top fund manager. Here is the tech giant he’s crowned.
news.summary: T. Rowe Price fund manager David Giroux says Big Tech isn’t in a bubble, but there’s value in healthcare and utilities.

news.title: Employers to college students: Never mind that 4.0 GPA. Go out and get a summer job.
news.summary: College students with any sort of work experience on their résumés are twice as likely to be employed shortly after graduating, according to a new study. The study found that college students with work experience are more likely to get a job.

news.title: Three powerful forces are draining family wealth — and your estate plan is completely unprepared
news.summary: From Medicaid cuts to an IRA tax trap, here are the real threats to your family’s inheritance and what to do about them.
